CAT.rationale: Valuation has compressed to near the low end of its 1-year PE range (14.96x vs 14.0–18.9x) while profitability remains exceptional (ROE 55.3%, operating margin 20.2%), but flat revenue growth and a persistent downtrend limit conviction.
key_factors: PE at 14.96x sits near the bottom of its 1y range (14.0–18.9x), a ~21% discount to the high end
ROE of 55.3% and operating margin of 20.2% indicate the business quality remains intact despite the selloff
Revenue is essentially flat quarter-over-quarter (~$15.8–16.2B), limiting the growth catalyst for re-rating
Stock down 9.6% in 3 months and 10.0% over 1 year, with no clear positive catalyst in recent news flow

DIS.rationale: DIS trades near the floor of its 1-year valuation range (28.6x vs 26.5–66.7x) after a 19% one-year decline, while the latest quarter shows sharp EPS improvement ($1.40 vs $0.26 prior), making the risk/reward mildly attractive but tempered by a persistent downtrend and modest returns.
key_factors: PE at 28.6x is near the low end of its 1y range of 26.5–66.7x, suggesting much of the de-rating is already priced in
Q1 FY2025 EPS jumped to $1.40 from $0.26 in the prior quarter, with revenue rising to $24.69B from $22.57B — clear fundamental improvement
Stock down 19.3% over 1 year and 11.5% over 3 months, closing at $98.70 — significant drawdown may limit further downside
Operating margin of 18.1% is solid, but net margin of 6.8% and ROE of 5.9% remain modest, capping upside enthusiasm

MRK.rationale: At 13.3x diluted PE with 26.7% net margins and 36.9% ROE, MRK is cheaply valued after a 32% one-year decline, but a wave of securities fraud lawsuits creates a meaningful near-term overhang that limits conviction.
key_factors: PE diluted at 13.3x — near the low end of its 1-year range (13.0–942.5), cheap for a pharma with 26.7% net margin and 36.9% ROE
Stock down 32% over the past year and 10% over 3 months, suggesting much negative news is already priced in
Multiple securities fraud class action lawsuits filed in March 2025 with lead plaintiff deadline April 14 — material legal uncertainty
News sentiment predominantly negative (net score -1.00 on five separate days in March), reflecting the lawsuit overhang

sources_cited.excerpt: The Gross Law Firm Reminds Merck Investors of the Pending Class Action Lawsuit with a Lead Plaintiff Deadline of April 14, 2025 – MRK

JNJ.rationale: Positive pipeline catalysts and strong fundamentals are largely offset by a near-peak 28.6x PE on flat revenue, leaving only a mild long lean.
key_factors: PE at 28.6x near top of 1y range (9.1–29.0) after +14.3% 3-month rally — valuation looks full
Revenue essentially flat at ~22.4–22.5B/quarter with FY2024 annual rev=88.82B showing no growth
Pipeline positives: lung cancer combo survival study (Mar 26) and FDA fast track for nipocalimab (Mar 18) support medium-term outlook
Solid margins (gross 69.1%, operating 18.8%, net 15.8%) and ROE 19.7% with D/E 0.45 provide fundamental floor

sources_cited.excerpt: J&J Drug Combo Outperforms Leading Lung Cancer Treatment In Survival Study
FDA Grants Fast Track Review To Johnson & Johnson's Nipocalimab For Autoimmune Disorder

GS.rationale: GS trades at a compressed 13.5x PE near the bottom of its one-year range after an ~18% six-week selloff, with strong FY24 earnings power offset by real macro/tariff headwinds that could persist but are increasingly priced in.
key_factors: PE at 13.5x near the low end of its 1y range (12.9–18.9), earnings yield ~7.4% on FY24 EPS of $40.54
Stock sold off ~18.7% from Feb 18 close of 672.19 to Mar 31 close of 546.29, potentially over-discounting macro risk
FY24 fundamentals solid: revenue $53.51B, net income $14.28B, net margin 26.7%, operating margin 34.4%
Negative macro/tariff news flow (Mar 4 bank plunge, Mar 16 tariff risk, Mar 20 market alarm) creates near-term overhang on capital markets and M&A activity

V.rationale: Visa's unmatched margin profile and ROE make it a quality compounder, and the recent ~9% pullback from late-Feb highs to mid-March lows improved the entry point, though modest revenue growth and lack of clear valuation metrics cap conviction.
key_factors: Exceptional profitability: net_margin=0.543, operating_margin=0.649, roe=0.522 — among the best in financials
Revenue modestly growing: Q1 FY25 rev=9.51B vs prior quarter 9.62B and 8.90B — sequential slight dip but YoY up
Stock pulled back from 362.71 (Feb 28) to 328.55 (Mar 13) then recovered to 350.46 — dip created better entry
News sentiment consistently positive (mostly +1.00 scores) with Visa framed as tariff-safe defensive hold

KO.rationale: KO trades at the top of its 1-year valuation range (29.1x PE) with declining quarterly revenue, making the risk/reward unattractive as defensive quality is already priced in.
key_factors: PE at top of 1y range: 29.1x vs 23.5-29.6x, after +17.1% 1y price run
Declining quarterly revenue: Q2 12.36B → Q3 11.85B → Q4 11.54B, not supporting multiple expansion
Strong but stable margins (net 22.6%, gross 61.1%, ROE 40.4%) limit upside surprise
News sentiment overwhelmingly positive (+1.00 on most days) but driven by generic Buffett/Berkshire association rather than KO-specific catalysts

TRV.rationale: Attractive risk-adjusted setup: PE near the low end of its own historical range (12.3x vs 11.1–18.1 band) combined with solid 17.9% ROE, improving quarterly EPS trajectory, and constructive price momentum offers more reward than risk over a one-year horizon.
key_factors: PE diluted at 12.3x, near the low end of its 1y range of 11.1–18.1, providing valuation cushion
ROE of 17.9% and operating margin of 13.3% reflect solid underwriting profitability
Quarterly EPS improving: Q2 $2.29 → Q3 $5.42 → Q4 $8.96, showing earnings momentum
Price momentum positive with 3m return +9.5% and 1y return +14.9%, closing at 264.46

JPM.rationale: A ~12% pullback has brought JPM to a reasonable 13x P/E with strong margins, but Dimon's own warning of economic weakening caps the upside and keeps the risk-reward only mildly positive.
key_factors: PE at 13.07x sits in lower-middle of its 1y range (10.4–14.9), reasonable after a ~12% pullback from ~280 to 245
Solid profitability: net_margin=0.31, ROE=0.155, operating_margin=0.398 support earnings power at current valuation
Dimon's March 12 warning of economic weakening and negative consumer sentiment introduces real macro/credit risk into the forward picture
Stock has partially recovered from March 10 low of 232.22 to 245.30, suggesting stabilization but not conviction-level reversal
1-year return of +22.5% with recent 3-month return of only +1.7% shows momentum has faded sharply

sources_cited.excerpt: Jamie Dimon Warns Of Economic Weakening, Negative Consumer Sentiment

AMGN.rationale: Extreme balance-sheet leverage (D/E=8.0) and a ~40x PE after an 18.6% three-month rally make risk outweigh reward despite a promising rocatinlimab Phase 3 readout.
key_factors: Extreme leverage: debt_to_equity=8.024 with total_debt=60.40B vs total_equity=7.53B, amplifying downside risk
Valuation stretched after rally: pe_diluted=39.92x near upper end of 1y range (21.0..58.3), stock up +18.6% in 3 months
Earnings volatility: quarterly EPS swung from -0.21 (Q1) to 1.38 (Q2) to 5.22 (Q3), making the ~40x multiple unreliable
Sentiment turning negative: net news score shifted from +1.00 to -1.00 on 2025-03-21 and 2025-03-25, with pharma sector selloff
Pipeline bright spot: rocatinlimab Phase 3 eczema data positive (2025-03-10), but not yet revenue-generating

sources_cited.excerpt: Amgen And Kyowa Kirin's Rocatinlimab Reduces Eczema Severity In Phase 3 Study

CRM.rationale: A 20.7% three-month sell-off has pushed CRM's PE to the bottom of its one-year range while quarterly revenue and EPS are both accelerating, making the risk-reward mildly attractive at current levels.
key_factors: Stock down 20.7% in 3 months to 268.36, with PE now at low end of 1y range (42.2x vs 77.7x high) — de-rating has created better entry
Quarterly fundamentals accelerating: revenue $9.32B→$9.44B→$9.99B and EPS $1.47→$1.58→$1.76 across last 3 quarters
Clean balance sheet with debt_to_equity=0.138 and strong gross_margin=0.772, operating_margin=0.19
Positive AI-driven news sentiment (Benioff touting $12T digital labor opportunity, multiple +1.00 sentiment days in March)

sources_cited.excerpt: Marc Benioff: Digital Labor Creating $12 Trillion Opportunity

WMT.rationale: WMT's sharp pullback from ~104 to ~88 creates a modest entry point for a defensive retailer with a strong balance sheet, but the 36.4x PE near the top of its 1-year range limits upside and leaves little margin of safety.
key_factors: PE diluted at 36.4x, near the high end of its 1-year range (27.1–39.1), expensive for a 3% net-margin retailer
Stock pulled back ~16% from Feb peak (~104 to 87.79), offering a better entry after a +45.9% 1-year run
Solid balance sheet: debt/equity=0.37, ROE=20.7%, providing defensive cushion amid rising recession fears
News sentiment net positive (March 18 bullish 'must-buy' article) but recession fears emerging March 22 add risk

sources_cited.excerpt: 3 Reasons Walmart Is a Must-Buy for Long-Term Investors
Recession Fears Escalate as Economists Identify Warning Signs

AAPL.rationale: A 13% pullback from highs and durable margins make AAPL modestly attractive, but a 35x PE against moderate growth leaves limited margin of safety, capping the reward relative to risk.
key_factors: PE at 35.0x sits mid-range of its 1y band (25.5–42.6x), still elevated for mid-single-digit revenue growth
Stock pulled back -13.1% in 3 months from ~247 to 222, improving entry point but not yet cheap
Fundamentals solid: net margin 24.3%, operating margin 31.8%, ROE 144%, but debt/equity 1.42 is notable
News flow tilts positive on AI optionality — 'Apple's Microsoft Moment' and potential Nvidia chip bet, sentiment net +0.75 on 3/31

sources_cited.excerpt: Is Apple About to Make a Massive Bet on Nvidia's AI Chips?

PG.rationale: PG trades near the low end of its recent valuation range with strong margins and solid YoY EPS growth, making it a defensively attractive hold with limited downside but no compelling catalyst.
key_factors: PE at 26.9x sits near the low end of its 1y range (25.7–30.8), offering relative valuation comfort
Strong profitability: ROE 30.3%, net margin 18.5%, gross margin 51.3%, operating margin 23.6%
Q2 FY25 EPS of 1.88 vs prior-year quarter 1.27 implies ~48% YoY EPS growth off a low base
Stock flat over 3 months (+0.5%) and modestly up 1 year (+5.0%), with mid-March dip to 162.85 recovered to 170.42 — limited downside for a defensive staple

VZ.rationale: Stock has rallied 13.6% in 3 months to trade near the top of its valuation range while EPS is declining, making the risk/reward unattractive at current levels.
key_factors: PE at 18.675x near top of 1y range (13.9–19.1), limited valuation upside after +13.6% 3-month rally
EPS deteriorated: Q3 2024 EPS=0.78 vs 1.09 in each of prior two quarters, a ~28% sequential decline
Revenue growth anemic: 33.33B vs 32.80B vs 32.98B across recent quarters — essentially flat
6.2% dividend yield attracts income buyers but news questions its safety (2025-03-20 headline)

sources_cited.excerpt: Is Verizon's 6.2%-Yielding Dividend Still Safe After This Announcement?

SHW.rationale: SHW trades at the lower end of its recent PE range with strong profitability, but Q4 EPS weakness and elevated leverage create offsetting risks that cap the reward-to-risk profile at a mild long lean.
key_factors: PE at 33.1x sits near the low end of its 1y range (30.9–39.7x), offering relative valuation support
Q4 2024 EPS of $1.90 marks a sharp decline from Q2's $3.50 and Q3's $3.18, signaling near-term earnings pressure
High leverage with debt-to-equity of 2.28 ($9.23B debt vs $4.05B equity) adds balance-sheet risk
Strong ROE of 66.2% and 48.5% gross margin underline durable franchise quality

AXP.rationale: A ~14% pullback has brought AXP's PE to the lower-middle of its one-year range while its 33.5% ROE and 26% net margin remain intact, making the risk-reward mildly attractive but not compelling given earnings deceleration in Q4 and elevated leverage.
key_factors: PE pulled back to 19.2x from 1y range high of 23.4x after a -10.6% 3-month drawdown, improving entry valuation
Strong profitability: ROE 33.5%, net margin 26.1%, operating margin 33.2% — premium franchise economics
17% dividend increase announced March 23 signals management confidence in earnings durability
Q4 EPS of 3.04 declined from Q2's 4.15 and Q3's 3.49, suggesting some earnings deceleration risk
Debt-to-equity at 1.64 adds balance-sheet risk in a higher-rate environment

sources_cited.excerpt: American Express Is Raising Its Dividend by an Impressive 17%

MCD.rationale: MCD's franchise quality (45% operating margins) and defensive dividend appeal support a premium multiple, but at 27.4x near the top of its range with only modest revenue growth, the risk/reward is only mildly positive.
key_factors: PE at 27.4x near top of 1y range (0.0-28.2), limiting upside from multiple expansion
Exceptional margins: operating_margin=45.2%, net_margin=31.7% support defensive quality premium
Modest revenue growth — FY2024 rev=25.92B with quarterly trajectory showing Q4 dip to 6.39B from Q3's 6.87B
Negative equity (-3.80B) and high debt (38.40B) from buybacks, but manageable given cash generation
Positive news sentiment (3/15, 3/24, 3/30 all +1.00) with dividend king and AI catalyst narratives

IBM.rationale: Trading at 38.7x earnings near the top of its one-year range, IBM's valuation demands AI-driven growth that its 9.6% net margin and modest 62.75B revenue have not yet delivered, making the risk of multiple compression outweigh the reward.
key_factors: PE diluted at 38.7x, near the top of its 1-year range (18.8–40.7), demanding significant growth not yet visible in financials
Revenue only 62.75B with net margin 9.6% and operating margin 9.2% — thin profitability for the current multiple
Stock already up +30.2% over the past year, with a recent pullback from 263 to 248 insufficient to reset valuation
News sentiment uniformly positive (+1.00 on most days) but largely generic market-size reports and a NVIDIA partnership — no IBM-specific earnings catalyst

sources_cited.excerpt: 2025-03-19 net sentiment +1.00 — NVIDIA Teams Up with GE, Google, IBM, And CrowdStrike To Redefine AI And Quantum Technology

CVX.rationale: Strong momentum and positive sentiment are offset by valuation near the top of its recent range and decelerating quarterly earnings, making the risk-reward only mildly favorable.
key_factors: PE at 17.2x near top of 1y range (13.0-17.6), limiting multiple-expansion upside
Q4 EPS declined to 1.84 from Q3's 2.48, signaling earnings deceleration despite stable revenue ~50-52B/qtr
3-month price rally of +16.2% to 167.29 may have front-run near-term upside
News sentiment overwhelmingly positive (all days +0.33 to +1.00) with acquisition catalyst and dividend appeal, but Trump Russian oil tariff threat adds geopolitical uncertainty

sources_cited.excerpt: 2025-03-30 — Trump Threatens Russian Oil Tariffs, Says He's 'Very Angry' At Putin
2025-03-19 — Chevron's High Confidence Is Driving It to Wager Over $2 Billion That It Will Close This Needle-Moving Acquisition

BA.rationale: Boeing's deeply negative gross margins, negative book equity, and 53.6B debt make the risk of ownership outweigh the speculative reward from F-47 catalysts and analyst optimism over a one-year horizon.
key_factors: Negative gross margin of -3% and operating margin of -16.1% in FY2024 mean Boeing is losing money on every plane before overhead — turnaround is not yet visible in the numbers
Total equity is negative at -3.91B with total debt of 53.62B, creating a precarious balance sheet with limited margin for error
F-47 defense contract win and analyst target of $204 are real catalysts, but revenue impact is years out; Q4 revenue of 15.24B declined from Q3's 17.84B
Stock rallied ~15% from March 10 low of 148.15 to 170.55 with improving news sentiment (+0.80 on Mar 21), but P/S of 1.659 only looks cheap if margins recover

sources_cited.excerpt: Boeing: Will the F-47 Prove to Be a Lifeline for the Stock?
Is Boeing Stock Going to $204? 1 Wall Street Analyst Thinks So.

CSCO.rationale: CSCO trades at 26.9x earnings — near the top of its 1-year range — for a company with tepid revenue growth and a sequential EPS decline, making the risk/reward unattractive after a 23.6% one-year run.
key_factors: PE at 26.9x near top of 1y range (14.2–28.3) despite low single-digit revenue growth (13.84B→13.99B QoQ)
Sequential EPS declined from $0.68 to $0.61 (~10% drop), undermining the re-rating narrative
Stock pulled back ~4.5% from Feb highs (64.59→61.71) after +23.6% 1y run, momentum fading
News sentiment uniformly +1.00 but driven by generic industry market-size articles, not company-specific catalysts
Solid fundamentals (net margin 17%, ROE 20.2%, debt/equity 0.44) provide downside support but don't justify premium multiple

MMM.rationale: After a 65.6% one-year rally, MMM's risk/reward is unattractive over the next year: Q4 EPS collapsed 46% sequentially to 1.33, leverage is elevated at 3.35x debt/equity, and news flags short-term pain ahead — all while the stock trades at a full 19.45x PE with limited margin of safety.
key_factors: Q4 EPS fell sharply to 1.33 from Q3's 2.48 — a 46% sequential decline signaling near-term earnings pressure
Stock already up 65.6% over past year; much of the post-litigation recovery appears priced in at PE 19.45x
High leverage with debt/equity=3.35 and total debt 13.04B vs equity 3.89B amplifies downside risk if earnings disappoint
News headline on 2025-03-19 explicitly flags 'potential short-term pain' alongside long-term gains

sources_cited.excerpt: 3M Looks Into the Future: Long-Term Gain, but Potential Short-Term Pain

HD.rationale: Declining quarterly EPS trajectory (4.60→3.67→3.01) combined with a still-elevated 24.6x PE and extreme leverage makes the risk-reward unattractive over a one-year horizon.
key_factors: Quarterly EPS declining sharply: Q2 $4.60 → Q3 $3.67 → Q4 $3.01, signaling deteriorating earnings power
PE at 24.58x is not cheap for a company with falling earnings, sitting mid-range of its 1y band (21.7–29.3x)
Extremely high leverage: debt/equity=7.74 with total_debt=$51.37B vs total_equity=$6.64B, amplifying downside risk if housing stays soft
Stock already down 6.7% in 3 months but no clear catalyst for reversal; analyst target of $445 (~21% upside) is the main bullish hook

sources_cited.excerpt: 1 Wall Street Analyst Thinks Home Depot Stock Is Going to $445

UNH.rationale: UNH trades at a near-range-high 33.8x PE on severely compressed margins (net 3.8%, operating 8.1%) with active legal investigations overhanging the stock — risk outweighs reward at current levels.
key_factors: PE at 33.8x sits near the top of its 1y range (18.4–38.4) on depressed earnings — net margin collapsed to 3.8% and operating margin to 8.1%
Multiple ongoing legal investigations (Rosen Law Firm fiduciary duty probe on 2025-03-09 and 2025-03-25; False Claims Act case on 2025-03-05) create overhang
Stock has recovered from Feb selloff (~466 to 524, +12%) but 1y return is only +5.9%, suggesting limited momentum edge
Gross margin remains strong at 88.3% and revenue scale at $400B provides downside support if margin recovery materializes

sources_cited.excerpt: 2025-03-25 — UNH Investors Are Encouraged to Contact The Rosen Law Firm About Ongoing Investigation of Breaches of Fiduciary Duties
2025-03-09 — UNH Investors Are Encouraged to Contact The Rosen Law Firm About Ongoing Investigation of Breaches of Fiduciary Duties
2025-03-05 — False Claims Act Case Against UnitedHealth Faces Dismissal Recommendation

HON.rationale: HON trades near the cheapest point in its recent valuation range with improving sequential revenue and active strategic catalysts, but elevated leverage (debt/equity 1.54) and choppy EPS temper the upside.
key_factors: PE at 22.91x near the low end of its 1-year range (20.8–25.5), with a March 20 article noting the stock is 'about as cheap as it's been since 2020'
Sequential revenue improving: 9.11B → 9.58B → 9.73B over the last three quarters, with strong 20.1% operating margin and 31.7% ROE
Strategic catalysts: $2B Sundyne acquisition (Mar 4) and breakup plans (Mar 20) signal portfolio optimization
Stock down 7.7% over 3 months despite positive news sentiment (consistently +1.00), creating a potential dislocation

sources_cited.excerpt: Honeywell's Stock Is About as Cheap as It's Been Since 2020. 1 Thing to Know Before You Buy.
Honeywell Isn't Going to Let a Little Breakup Ruin Its Growth Plans
Honeywell beefs up energy security offerings with $2 billion Sundyne deal

MSFT.rationale: MSFT has pulled back ~13% over three months to the bottom of its own 1-year valuation range (PE 31x vs 31–40.5x) while its profitability and balance sheet remain best-in-class, making the risk-reward mildly attractive over a one-year horizon.
key_factors: PE at 30.98x sits at the very bottom of its 1y range (31.0–40.5x), and EV/EBIT at 24.5x is likewise at the floor of its 24.5–32.7x range — valuation compression after a 12.8% 3-month pullback
Fortress fundamentals: net margin 35.6%, operating margin 44.5%, ROE 31.5%, debt/equity 0.157, and cash 78.43B exceeds total debt 45.12B
Revenue grew ~6% YoY (61.86B→65.58B) with EPS up ~12% (2.94→3.30), showing operating leverage despite moderate top-line growth
News sentiment consistently positive (most recent days +0.75 to +1.00, latest +0.80 on Mar 31), no negative catalysts visible in the data

AMZN.rationale: AMZN has de-rated to the bottom of its 1-year PE range (34.4x) on a broad Nasdaq sell-off while fundamentals keep accelerating — the risk-reward over one year leans attractive but the downtrend and still-elevated multiple cap conviction.
key_factors: PE at 34.4x — the very bottom of its 1-year range (34.4–65.2x), a sharp de-rating from recent levels
Accelerating quarterly EPS: 1.26→1.43→1.86 across Q2–Q4 2024 with operating margin at 10.8% and net margin 9.3%
15% pullback in 3 months appears driven by broad Nasdaq sell-off (Nvidia cited losing ~$800B market cap) rather than AMZN-specific deterioration
Conservative balance sheet: debt-to-equity 0.203, total debt 58.0B vs equity 285.97B
News sentiment remained net positive through March (scores +0.29 to +1.00) even as price fell, suggesting fundamental story intact

sources_cited.excerpt: Nasdaq Sell-Off: After Losing Nearly $800 Billion in Market Cap, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy Anymore?

NKE.rationale: Nike's 32.5% one-year selloff has compressed PE to the bottom of its range for a high-ROE franchise, but ongoing inventory liquidation and declining revenue/EPS mean the fundamental trough is not yet confirmed, making this a mild long with limited conviction.
key_factors: PE at bottom of 1y range (19.36x vs 19.1–28.4x) after -32.5% 1y price decline, compressing valuation to attractive levels for a 34.8% ROE franchise
Revenue and EPS declining: Q2 rev 12.35B vs prior-year Q4 12.61B, EPS 0.78 vs 0.99 — fundamental deterioration still underway
News flow confirms 'slow turn' with ongoing inventory liquidation (Mar 25) and negative late-March sentiment (-1.00 on Mar 23/24), suggesting near-term headwinds persist
Gross margin 44.6% and operating margin 12% show brand strength intact, but tariff uncertainty (Mar 24 news) adds risk to cost structure

sources_cited.excerpt: Nike Earnings: Expect a Slow Turn as Inventory Liquidation Continues
US Stocks Likely To Open Higher As Trump May Soften Tariff Plans

NVDA.rationale: Exceptional fundamentals (55.8% net margin, $130.5B revenue, 36.9x PE) combined with a 20.9% three-month drawdown create a mildly attractive entry, but deteriorating sentiment and a persistent downtrend warrant only a cautious lean.
key_factors: Revenue of $130.5B with 55.8% net margin and 62.4% operating margin — best-in-class profitability at scale
PE diluted at 36.9x after a -20.9% 3-month selloff, a reasonable multiple for this growth profile
News sentiment deteriorating from +0.80 (3/17) to +0.14 (3/31) with headlines citing ~$800B market cap loss
Clean balance sheet with debt/equity of 0.107 and ROE of 91.9%

sources_cited.excerpt: After Losing Nearly $800 Billion in Market Cap, Is Nvidia Stock a Buy Anymore?

trace.final_explanation: LLM-only baseline: 30/30 views on 2025-04-01 via z-ai/glm-5.2.
